theme: bell-ringer arranging chimes to minimize arm travel
title: Bell-Tower Chime Rack: Minimizing Mallet Travel
strategies: trivial: canonical ID-order near-balanced code (checker's own baseline construction; ignores strike frequency and strike order entirely)

greedy (trap): classic Huffman coding on per-chime strike counts (optimizes depth-vs-frequency in isolation) but assigns leaf positions with no regard to which chimes strike back-to-back, so co-occurring chimes end up scattered across the rack

strong: builds an undirected chime-adjacency graph from consecutive strike pairs, orders chimes via a max-weight nearest-neighbor chain (greedy graph traversal favoring frequently-co-occurring chimes), then recursively bisects that order into a balanced binary rack -- embedding the transition graph into the tree metric instead of only balancing depth by frequency
invalid: emits the same single address for every chime (violates both uniqueness and the prefix-free constraint)
